response.justification: Bogdan has genuine GIS and geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrates breadth across many environments, but his career is firmly rooted in web/full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js). His C++ exposure is narrow and dated — a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 writing components for a C IDE, plus a bachelor thesis project — which falls well short of the commercial-grade modern C++ (or Rust) proficiency this role demands. There is no evidence of experience with routing data pipelines, navigation tile generation, or the low-level systems/distributed-systems depth (fault tolerance, consistency protocols, service discovery) central to this position.
strengths: Hands-on GIS/geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map web GIS application spanning multiple years
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby using DLang with async I/O and fiber-based concurrency at millions of requests/second
Broad polyglot background (DLang, C/C++, Java, TypeScript) shows willingness to expand across the stack
concerns: C++ experience is limited to a 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis; no commercial modern C++ or any Rust experience evident
Career trajectory is predominantly web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, GraphQL) — no routing pipeline, navigation tile, or embedded geospatial systems work
No evidence of distributed-systems depth relevant to this role: fault tolerance design, consistency guarantees, or service discovery at infrastructure level
